input.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

payload.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

source_payload.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

pinterest_cluster_labels: light-wood Japandi dining — round table + woven pendant
floor-level tea nook — floor cushions + bamboo screens
neutral Japandi living — low sofa, sculptural wooden coffee table

pins.title: Japandi Dining Room with Neutral Decor
pins.description: A Japandi dining room with neutral décor blends Japanese simplicity and Scandinavian warmth. Soft beige, warm wood, and gentle earth tones create a calm, inviting space. Clean lines, natural materials, and minimal furnishings keep the room feeling open and uncluttered, while subtle textures like linen, ceramic, and light wood add quiet elegance and comfort.
pins.cluster_label: light-wood Japandi dining — round table + woven pendant

pins.title: Japandi nook area
pins.cluster_label: floor-level tea nook — floor cushions + bamboo screens

pins.title: Japandi Living Room: Calm Minimal Decor, Natural Wood & Indoor Plants
pins.description: Calm Japandi living room inspiration with a light neutral sofa, low wooden coffee table, soft textured rug, and plenty of indoor plants for a serene, balanced space. Minimal wall art, natural materials, and clean lines create an airy, soothing aesthetic—perfect for Japanese style interiors, minimalist living room ideas, and warm neutral home decor. Save for Japandi decor and plant-filled living room styling.
pins.cluster_label: neutral Japandi living — low sofa, sculptural wooden coffee table

cluster_summary.label: light-wood Japandi dining — round table + woven pendant
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Round oak dining table, curved mid-height upholstered chairs in linen textures, woven rattan pendant for warm upward glow; staging uses ceramic tableware, single sculptural vase and subtle wall wood slatting to elongate vertical lines.

cluster_summary.label: floor-level tea nook — floor cushions + bamboo screens
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Intimate floor seating with oversized cushioned loungers, layered natural fiber rugs and bamboo/shoji screening; low wooden tray table, ambient paper lanterns and small green plantings to create a cozy, meditative corner.

cluster_summary.label: neutral Japandi living — low sofa, sculptural wooden coffee table
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Light neutral linen sofa on low wooden base, asymmetrical sculpted oak coffee table, textured area rug and potted greenery; pared-back art and vertical wood slat accents emphasize calm proportion and organic materiality.

Other room sizes and some window placements/adjacencies inferred from floor plan graphic; small service closets, pantry and hall labeled as 'other'. Window positions along exterior walls inferred where bay/glass elements are drawn (Dine, Relax, Nest front).

vibe_report.summary: A calm, warm Japandi interior for a young family: minimal, textured, and practical. Light oak, soft linen, and sculpted timber form the backbone of the look. Keep furnishings low and grounded, layered natural textiles for tactile comfort, and intentional negative space for a meditative feel. Include a compact, ergonomic standing-desk work corner in the main living area and a small crib in the primary bedroom—both integrated so they read as natural parts of the layout rather than afterthoughts.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: Warm-neutrals anchored by light oak and muted stone tones create a soothing, family-friendly backdrop. Use brighter warm accents selectively (ceramic ochres, deep taupe) to highlight functional zones (dining, work corner) while preserving an overall calm monochrome field.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Soft, directional daylight from the front-facing windows and glass doors with warm practical fixtures (woven rattan pendant, floor lamp). Emphasize believable sunlight shafts and subtle shadow modeling from visible windows; ambient fills by low-wattage warm pendants and table lamps.
materials: warm-toned plaster or matte eggshell paint
woven rattan pendant and bamboo screens
furniture_language: rounded oak dining table and curved-back chairs
slim-profile standing desk with integrated cable management
simple wooden crib with rounded corners
styling_rules: Keep verticals perfectly preserved in image passes — avoid slanted walls or perspective drift.
Stage the work corner as a tidy, family-ready appliance: cable-hidden monitor, simple task lamp, one active notebook; no clutter.
Place crib against an interior wall away from direct drafts; keep bedding neutral and minimal.
Use one sculptural object per horizontal surface (e.g., single ceramic vase on dining table).
Limit decorative pillows and textiles to two layered textures maximum per seating area.
avoid: over-staged toy clutter or visible child gear in hero shots
light sources that cannot be physically explained by windows/fixtures

room_guidance.headline: Calm multi-use living + compact standing-desk corner
room_guidance.guidance: Compose a wide, low-angle view that emphasizes the low sofa, sculptural coffee table and the standing-desk corner tucked near the front window. Keep the desk visually integrated: slim oak desk, neutral monitor, soft task lamp. Allow clear sightline to the patio glass doors to show indoor-outdoor connection.
must_include: standing desk work corner with slim task lamp
woven rattan pendant or floor lamp

room_guidance.headline: Serene bedroom with family-ready crib
room_guidance.guidance: Keep the bed low and central; place a compact crib on one side close to the interior wall. Use layered linens and a wool rug for warmth. Emphasize calming north light through the window; include a small bedside pendant or sconce for warm practical lighting.
must_avoid: bright nursery themes or busy prints
crib directly in front of the window

room_guidance.headline: Quiet, tactile kitchen that reads into the dining bay
room_guidance.guidance: Show material details (matte oak cabinetry, ceramic sink, muted hardware) and a clean pass-through sightline into the dining bay. Keep surfaces clear except for a single wooden cutting board and a ceramic pitcher.
must_include: soft pendant or under-cabinet practical lighting

room_guidance.headline: Light-wood round dining bay under woven pendant
room_guidance.guidance: Center the round oak table beneath a woven rattan pendant. Use three curved linen chairs and a single sculptural vase as the table centerpiece. Capture daylight through the bay windows and a warm pendant glow for evening variants.

room_guidance.headline: Floor-level tea nook and indoor-outdoor reading spot
room_guidance.guidance: Create an intimate floor-level composition with layered cushions, small low tray table, and bamboo/shoji screen as backdrop. Keep green plantings modest and natural. Ensure the glass doors read as the primary light source.

warnings: Several ancillary rooms lack precise measured dimensions; rely on inferred scale from primary rooms when staging.
Do not introduce unseen architectural changes; preserve door/window positions from floor plan.

creative_spec.overall_mood: Calm meditative warmth — quiet, natural, and family-ready
global_style_notes: Preserve exact architecture and layout in all image passes; correct perspective but maintain composition.
Derive all illumination from visible windows and tangible fixtures. No invented directional light.
Prioritize natural materials and tactile surfaces in closeups; balance wide environmental shots with 1–2 detail frames per room.
Keep presence of family items minimal and purposeful (crib, standing desk) and stage them to appear used but tidy.
creative_spec.negative_prompt: blur, distort, low quality, warped architecture, flicker, unstable camera, unrealistic lighting, muddy shadows, layout drift
